title: crucifera supina
intro: Crucifera supina, also known as False Flax or Hack's Tea, is a flowering plant belonging to the family Brassicaceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The plant is valued for its health benefits, such as relieving digestive issues, reducing fever, and soothing respiratory problems. The herbal preparations of Crucifera supina include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made by steeping the leaves and flowers in hot water or mixing them with a solvent like ethanol.
